title: cordia elaeagnoides
intro: Cordia elaeagnoides, also known as 'Barkha' or 'Wild Pear', is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial and antifungal activities, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cordia elaeagnoides include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and skin infections. In traditional herbal medicine, Cordia elaeagnoides is often prepared in various forms, including infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
